suite.name: Test Ebgp

test.name: Lab Should Start Successfully

kw.name: Netlab Up
Netlab Up
kw.msg: ${stdout} = Creating configuration files [CONFIG] spine1: normalize,initial,bgp,spine,bfd [CONFIG] spine2: normalize,initial,bgp,spine,bfd [CONFIG] leaf1: normalize,initial,bgp,leaf,bfd [CONFIG] leaf2: normal...
kw.doc: Run `netlab up --no-config` to start containers, then push configs manually.

kw.name: Should Be Equal As Integers
kw.doc: Fails if objects are unequal after converting them to integers.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.
Start the lab via `netlab up`.

kw.name: Sleep
kw.msg: Wait for BGP sessions to establish
kw.arg: Wait for BGP sessions to establish
kw.doc: Pauses the test executed for the given time.

test.doc: Deploy the lab via netlab up and wait for BGP to stabilize.

test.name: All EOS Nodes Should Run Arista cEOS

kw.name: Verify All EOS Nodes Respond
Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg:
${output} = Arista cEOSLab Hardware version: Serial number: BD65C492E38FD5AD3DA6EF8E78EC56D4 Hardware MAC address: 001c.73ab.e315 System MAC address: 001c.73ab.e315
Software image version: 4.34.2.2F-46218727.43...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg:
${output} = Arista cEOSLab Hardware version: Serial number: BD65C492E38FD5AD3DA6EF8E78EC56D4 Hardware MAC address: 001c.73ab.e315 System MAC address: 001c.73ab.e315
Software image version: 4.34.2.2F-46218727.43...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

Run Show Command

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.
Verify all EOS nodes respond with correct version.

test.name: All Data Interfaces Should Be Up On Spine Nodes

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${up_count} = ['Ethernet1 10.2.1.0/31 up up', 'Ethernet2 10.2.1.2/31 up up', 'Ethernet3 10.2.1.4/31 up up']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${up_count} = ['Ethernet1 10.2.2.0/31 up up', 'Ethernet2 10.2.2.2/31 up up', 'Ethernet3 10.2.2.4/31 up up']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

test.doc: Verify all Ethernet interfaces on spine nodes are up/up.

test.name: All Data Interfaces Should Be Up On Leaf Nodes

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${up_count} = ['Ethernet1 10.2.1.1/31 up up', 'Ethernet2 10.2.2.1/31 up up', 'Ethernet3 10.3.1.0/31 up up']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: ${node} should have 3 Ethernet interfaces up/up (2 spine + 1 host)
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${up_count} = ['Ethernet1 10.2.1.3/31 up up', 'Ethernet2 10.2.2.3/31 up up', 'Ethernet3 10.3.2.0/31 up up']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: ${node} should have 3 Ethernet interfaces up/up (2 spine + 1 host)
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${up_count} = ['Ethernet1 10.2.1.5/31 up up', 'Ethernet2 10.2.2.5/31 up up', 'Ethernet3 10.3.3.0/31 up up']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: ${node} should have 3 Ethernet interfaces up/up (2 spine + 1 host)
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

test.doc: Verify all Ethernet interfaces on leaf nodes are up/up.

kw.name: Length Should Be

test.doc: Verify Loopback0 interface status is up/up.

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Be Established On Spine1

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:11, last write 00:00:53 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:11, last write 00:00:53 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${established_count} = ['BGP state is Established', 'BGP state is Established', 'BGP state is Established']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: spine1 should have 3 Established BGP sessions
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

test.doc: spine1 should have 3 eBGP sessions to leaf1, leaf2, leaf3.

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Be Established On Spine2

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.2.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:14, last write 00:00:11 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.2.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:14, last write 00:00:11 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: spine2 should have 3 Established BGP sessions
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

test.doc: spine2 should have 3 eBGP sessions to leaf1, leaf2, leaf3.

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Be Established On Leaf1

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.0, remote AS 65001, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.1.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:55, last write 00:00:12 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60 ...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.0, remote AS 65001, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.1.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:55, last write 00:00:12 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60 ...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${established_count} = ['BGP state is Established', 'BGP state is Established']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: leaf1 should have 2 Established BGP sessions
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

test.doc: leaf1 should have 2 eBGP sessions to spine1 and spine2.

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Be Established On Leaf2

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.2, remote AS 65001, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.1.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:13, last write 00:00:13 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60 ...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.2, remote AS 65001, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.1.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:13, last write 00:00:13 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60 ...

test.doc: leaf2 should have 2 eBGP sessions to spine1 and spine2.

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Be Established On Leaf3

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.4, remote AS 65001, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.1.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:56, last write 00:00:14 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60 ...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.4, remote AS 65001, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.1.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:56, last write 00:00:14 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60 ...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${established_count} = ['BGP state is Established', 'BGP state is Established']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.arg: leaf3 should have 2 Established BGP sessions

test.doc: leaf3 should have 2 eBGP sessions to spine1 and spine2.

test.name: BGP Should Distribute Routes On Spine1

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.1.1, local AS number 65001 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.1.1, local AS number 65001 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine1 should have BGP route to spine2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine1 should have BGP route to leaf1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine1 should have BGP route to leaf2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine1 should have BGP route to leaf3
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.doc: spine1 should have BGP routes to spine2 and all leaf loopbacks.

test.name: BGP Should Distribute Routes On Spine2

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.2.1, local AS number 65002 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.2.1, local AS number 65002 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine2 should have BGP route to spine1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine2 should have BGP route to leaf1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine2 should have BGP route to leaf2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine2 should have BGP route to leaf3
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.doc: spine2 should have BGP routes to spine1 and all leaf loopbacks.

test.name: BGP Should Distribute Routes On Leaf1

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.11.1, local AS number 65101 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.11.1, local AS number 65101 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf1 should have BGP route to spine1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf1 should have BGP route to spine2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf1 should have BGP route to leaf2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf1 should have BGP route to leaf3
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.doc: leaf1 should have BGP routes to spines and other leaves.

test.name: BGP Should Distribute Routes On Leaf2

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.12.1, local AS number 65102 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf2 should have BGP route to spine1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf2 should have BGP route to spine2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf2 should have BGP route to leaf1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf2 should have BGP route to leaf3

test.doc: leaf2 should have BGP routes to spines and other leaves.

test.name: BGP Should Distribute Routes On Leaf3

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.13.1, local AS number 65103 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP routing table information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.13.1, local AS number 65103 Route status codes: s - suppressed contributor, * - valid, > - active, E - ECMP head, e - ECMP ...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf3 should have BGP route to spine1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf3 should have BGP route to spine2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf3 should have BGP route to leaf1
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: leaf3 should have BGP route to leaf2
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.doc: leaf3 should have BGP routes to spines and other leaves.

test.name: Ping Spine1 To Leaf1 Loopback Should Succeed

kw.name: Run Ping Command
Run Ping
kw.msg: ${output} = PING 10.0.11.1 (10.0.11.1) from 10.0.1.1 : 72(100) bytes of data. 80 bytes from 10.0.11.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.060 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.11.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=0.017 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.1...
kw.doc: Ping from a node via `netlab connect` (no show prefix).

kw.msg: ${output} = PING 10.0.11.1 (10.0.11.1) from 10.0.1.1 : 72(100) bytes of data. 80 bytes from 10.0.11.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.060 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.11.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=0.017 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.1...
kw.doc: Ping from a node via `netlab connect`.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.name: Ping Spine1 To Leaf3 Loopback Should Succeed

kw.name: Run Ping Command
Run Ping
kw.msg: ${output} = PING 10.0.13.1 (10.0.13.1) from 10.0.1.1 : 72(100) bytes of data. 80 bytes from 10.0.13.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=64 time=0.062 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.13.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=64 time=0.019 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.1...
kw.doc: Ping from a node via `netlab connect` (no show prefix).

kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Run Ping Command
Run Ping
kw.msg: ${output} = PING 10.0.2.1 (10.0.2.1) from 10.0.1.1 : 72(100) bytes of data. 80 bytes from 10.0.2.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=63 time=0.644 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.2.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=63 time=0.372 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.2.1: ...

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.name: Ping Leaf1 To Leaf2 Loopback Should Succeed

Run Ping
kw.msg: ${output} = PING 10.0.12.1 (10.0.12.1) from 10.0.11.1 : 72(100) bytes of data. 80 bytes from 10.0.12.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=63 time=1.11 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.12.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=63 time=0.514 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.1...

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

Run Ping
kw.msg: ${output} = PING 10.0.13.1 (10.0.13.1) from 10.0.11.1 : 72(100) bytes of data. 80 bytes from 10.0.13.1: icmp_seq=1 ttl=63 time=0.922 ms 80 bytes from 10.0.13.1: icmp_seq=2 ttl=63 time=0.529 ms 80 bytes from 10.0....

kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.doc: Show routing table on a Linux host container.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Run Host Ping
kw.doc: Ping from a Linux host container via docker exec.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.doc: Cross-host ping via the fabric.

test.name: BFD Should Be Configured On Spine Interfaces

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 3 BFD peers configured
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 3 BFD peers configured
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

test.doc: Verify BFD peers are configured on all Ethernet interfaces of spine nodes.

test.name: BFD Should Be Configured On Leaf Interfaces

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 2 BFD peers configured
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 2 BFD peers configured
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 2 BFD peers configured
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

test.doc: Verify BFD peers are configured on spine-facing interfaces of leaf nodes.

test.name: BFD Sessions Should Be Up On Spine Nodes

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 3 BFD sessions up
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 3 BFD sessions up
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

test.doc: Verify BFD sessions are established on spine nodes.

test.name: BFD Sessions Should Be Up On Leaf Nodes

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 2 BFD sessions up (to spines)
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 2 BFD sessions up (to spines)
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.
Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Should Be True
kw.arg: ${node} should have at least 2 BFD sessions up (to spines)
kw.doc: Fails if the given condition is not true.

test.doc: Verify BFD sessions are established on leaf nodes (spine-facing interfaces).

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Have BFD Enabled On Spine1

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:30, last write 00:00:13 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:30, last write 00:00:13 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine1 BGP neighbor ${neighbor_ip} should be present
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine1 BGP neighbor ${neighbor_ip} should be present
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine1 BGP neighbor ${neighbor_ip} should be present
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${bfd_count} = ['BFD is enabled', 'BFD is enabled', 'BFD is enabled']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: spine1 should have BFD enabled for 3 BGP neighbors
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

test.doc: Verify BFD is enabled for all BGP neighbors on spine1.

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Have BFD Enabled On Spine2

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.2.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:33, last write 00:00:29 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.2.1, remote AS 65101, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.11.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:33, last write 00:00:29 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine2 BGP neighbor ${neighbor_ip} should be present
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine2 BGP neighbor ${neighbor_ip} should be present
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.arg: spine2 BGP neighbor ${neighbor_ip} should be present
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

kw.name: Get Regexp Matches
kw.msg: ${bfd_count} = ['BFD is enabled', 'BFD is enabled', 'BFD is enabled']
kw.doc: Returns a list of all non-overlapping matches in the given string.

kw.name: Length Should Be
kw.arg: spine2 should have BFD enabled for 3 BGP neighbors
kw.doc: Verifies that the length of the given item is correct.

test.doc: Verify BFD is enabled for all BGP neighbors on spine2.

test.name: BGP Neighbors Should Have BFD Enabled On Leaf Nodes

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP neighbor is 10.2.1.0, remote AS 65001, external link BGP version 4, remote router ID 10.0.1.1, VRF default Last read 00:00:15, last write 00:00:31 Hold time is 180, keepalive interval is 60 ...

Run Show Command

kw.name: Length Should Be

test.doc: Verify BFD is enabled for BGP neighbors on all leaf nodes.

test.name: BGP Router-ID Should Be Set On All EOS Nodes

test.doc: Verify BGP router-id matches the loopback IP on all nodes.

test.name: BGP Address-Family IPv4 Should Be Active On Spine Nodes

kw.name: Run Show Command
Run Show Command
kw.msg: ${output} = BGP summary information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.1.1, local AS number 65001 Neighbor AS Session State AFI/SAFI AFI/SAFI State NLRI Rcd NLRI Acc NLRI Adv ---...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP summary information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.13.1, local AS number 65103 Neighbor AS Session State AFI/SAFI AFI/SAFI State NLRI Rcd NLRI Acc NLRI Adv --...
kw.doc: Keyword alias for netlab_connect_show.

kw.msg: ${output} = BGP summary information for VRF default Router identifier 10.0.13.1, local AS number 65103 Neighbor AS Session State AFI/SAFI AFI/SAFI State NLRI Rcd NLRI Acc NLRI Adv --...
kw.doc: Run `netlab connect <node> --show <cmd>` and return output.

kw.name: Should Contain
kw.doc: Fails if ``container`` does not contain ``item`` one or more times.

test.doc: Verify address-family ipv4 unicast is configured on leaf nodes.

test.name: Lab Should Tear Down Successfully

kw.name: Netlab Down
Netlab Down
kw.msg: ${stdout} = Checking virtualization provider installation OK: clab installed and working correctly Stopping clab nodes

${stderr} = 23:20:50 INFO Parsing & checking topology file=clab.yml 23:20:50 INFO Parsing & checking topology file=clab.yml 23:20:50 INFO Destroying lab name=netlab 23:20:51 INFO Removed container name=clab-netla...
kw.doc: Run `netlab down` to tear down the lab.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.
Tear down the lab via `netlab down`.

test.doc: Tear down the lab via netlab down.

kw.name: Netlab Down
Netlab Down
kw.msg: ${stdout} = Checking virtualization provider installation OK: clab installed and working correctly Stopping clab nodes
${stderr} = 23:20:52 INFO Parsing & checking topology file=clab.yml 23:20:52 INFO no containerlab containers found
kw.doc: Run `netlab down` to tear down the lab.

kw.name: Run Keyword If
kw.doc: Runs the given keyword with the given arguments, if ``condition`` is true.
Tear down the lab via `netlab down`.

suite.doc: Runtime tests for eBGP underlay — validates: - Lab lifecycle (netlab up / netlab down) - Node version and reachability - Interface status (up/up) - BGP neighbors (Established) - BGP route exchange (all loopbacks learned) - BFD configuration and sessions - BGP router-id and address-family - End-to-end ping (spine-to-leaf, leaf-to-leaf, leaf-to-spine, host-to-all)
